untranslatable_reason: This signal describes an options calendar spread strategy with parameters (strike_price, near-term expiration, longer-term expiration, net credit) that are not directly expressible using price action indicators. The strategy requires: (1) tracking two separate option positions with different expirations, (2) monitoring option Greeks and time decay, (3) executing on specific calendar dates, and (4) measuring profit relative to net credit received—none of which can be represented with the allowed price/volume/oscillator indicators. The exit condition 'stock exactly at striking price' cannot be reliably triggered by price equality due to tick size and market microstructure. A backtest engine would need options-specific data and Greeks (delta, theta, vega) to properly model this spread.
translation_notes: Calendar spread strategies operate in the derivatives space and require options chain data, Greeks calculations, and time decay modeling. This signal is fundamentally incompatible with price-action-only backtesting frameworks.
